reachability_artifacts: sworn run --task <fixture> --base <non-default-ref> through the built binary, recording selected base, created branch, terminal verdict, and exit code
sworn run --parallel --release <malformed-fixture> through the built binary, recording zero worker dispatches and non-zero exit

effort_complexity.rationale: See spec.json; implementer must confirm which task engine becomes authoritative before design approval.

release_benefit: A SwornAgent operator can trust a recoverable autonomous release loop, receive durable attention pages, and monitor or safely control it from a mobile web board.

positive_scenarios: Task mode uses a non-default base through the same engine tested directly.
Parallel mode routes all work from committed Baton state.
negative_scenarios: A malformed board/status cannot trigger a retired worker fallback.
No accepted run flag is parsed and discarded.
validation.benefit_hypothesis: One production execution authority makes unit evidence predictive of the real CLI and eliminates optimistic fallback routing.

design_decisions.choice: Make cmd/sworn a thin adapter over one task engine and one router-driven parallel engine.
options: choose and wire one task engine
design_decisions.rationale: Tests and production must exercise the same orchestration code for reachability evidence to be meaningful.

design_decisions.choice: Reject router construction failure without a production fallback.
design_decisions.rationale: Unknown committed state cannot safely imply an implementation role.
